title: deny_unknown_fields error doesn't name the offending field in nested transport table
body: ClientServiceConfig (and friends) in config.rs are annotated with `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]`, which is great for catching typos, but when the typo is inside a nested `[client.transport]` table the resulting toml deserialize error just says an unknown field exists somewhere without pinpointing which table/key, making it painful to spot in a large config.

title: MaskedString only hides secrets from Debug, not from dumped/generated configs
body: MaskedString's Debug impl prints "MASKED" so logs look safe, but when the effective config is serialized back out (e.g. for a `--dump-config` style feature) it still round-trips through Serialize using the real string. Should the masking also apply anywhere the config gets written back to disk or stdout, not just logging?

title: WebSocket transport doesn't forward custom headers for authenticated reverse proxies
body: We're running rathole's client behind an authenticating reverse proxy in front of the server (websocket transport). transport/websocket.rs's connect path doesn't appear to let us attach an Authorization or custom header to the initial WebSocket upgrade request, so the proxy rejects the handshake before rathole's own auth even runs.

title: Unclear hot-reload behavior when changing transport_type at runtime
body: config_watcher.rs clearly supports reloading service-level changes without restarting, but it's not documented whether changing `transport_type` from tcp to noise in the config file while rathole is running actually takes effect, or requires a full restart of the client/server process. Could this be documented explicitly?

title: Control channel reconnect backoff is too aggressive against flaky links
body: On a client with an intermittently flaky uplink, run_control_chan_backoff in client.rs seems to retry frequently enough that when many rathole clients share the same unstable network segment, the server sees a thundering herd of reconnect attempts. Could the backoff constants in constants.rs add more jitter or a higher ceiling?
